items.project_definition: The set of v1 endpoints hosted at localhost:8000 serving as the primary interface for system interaction.
A logic gate preventing the removal of a learning resource if it currently possesses linked student associations.
The verification process using bearer tokens passed within the authorization header.
An asynchronous operation used for sending welcome emails without delaying the HTTP response.
The operational logic determining which data is returned based on the requester's identity and resource publication status.
Specific organizational constraints that dictate successful or failed state transitions for domain entities.
The cross-origin resource sharing protocol implied by the base URL and endpoint structure for browser-based access.
A learning entity containing a title, price in cents, and a collection of instructional modules.
The one-way transformation process using the bcrypt algorithm to secure user passwords.
The association between a learner and a specific learning resource, tracking completion percentage.
A structured payload containing a status code, a timestamp, and a list of failure details.
A relational database pointer linking one entity's primary identifier to another's.
The 8601 standard utilized for consistent representation of timestamps across all date fields.
The primary data interchange format specified for all request and response bodies.
Compact, URL-safe signed tokens used for identity propagation and access control.
A sequential component of a learning resource defined by a title and a numerical position.
The logical negation used to specify that a side effect must not block the primary execution flow.
The logical disjunction allowing access if either the resource is public or the user is the owner.
The specific programming language used as the subject matter for the sample educational content.
Optional URLK-value pairs used for pagination, specifically skip and limit integers.
The incoming HTTP payload containing the necessary data to trigger a specific server operation.
The server's output consisting of a status code and a structured data envelope.
The classification of a user account, restricted to either student or instructor, determining permission levels.
The primary account entity containing a unique email, display name, and security credentials.
The 128-bit universally unique identifier used as the primary key for all entities.
